_meta.description: Persistent living knowledge for the Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor (SCEM). Carries forward conflict baselines (indicator medians build over 13 weeks), F-flag history, and roster status across weekly issues.

conflict_baselines.notes: All indicators at structural wartime norm. Spring offensive staging detected (I2 trajectory signal).

version_history.change: Initial baseline seeding from Week 1 scoring

conflict_baselines.notes: Ceasefire holds at low friction. F4 flag on I5 — ceasefire claims unverified. Iran conflict side-effects (crossing closures) constitute I4 signal.

conflict_baselines.conflict: US–Israel vs Iran (Op Epic Fury / Roaring Lion)
conflict_baselines.roster_note: Retroactive correction — active since 28 February 2026. Placed in Roster Watch in error in inaugural edition.
conflict_baselines.notes: Three indicators at RED-band deviation levels under Contested Baseline. I3: 400kg enriched uranium at unknown location. I4: Hormuz closure — largest oil disruption in recorded history. F3 on CENTCOM 'dismantled' claim; F4 on Iran conditional mediation signals.

version_history.reason: Omission error in inaugural edition — conflict active since 28 Feb 2026

f_flag_history.detail: Kremlin negotiating signals T3 only. No T2 corroboration.
Ceasefire/disarmament progress claims from Israeli/US officials (T3). Hamas disarmament unverified.
Doha/Washington/Angola ceasefire claims unverified operationally. MONUSCO oversight not functional.
Mutual drone attribution without forensic corroboration. Pattern consistent with false-flag seeding.
'Limited and targeted' Israeli framing inconsistent with operational scale (second division, Litani buffer zone, 700+ sorties).
CENTCOM claim of Iranian capabilities 'totally dismantled' contradicted by ongoing operations, Hormuz closure, Gulf attacks.
Iran conditional mediation signals (29 March) contradicted by FM denial of negotiations same day (31 March Al Jazeera).
